title: libpod/events: Update event time format and add timeNano
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Fixes #14993

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

Yes

```release-note
The `Time` field in the output of `podman events --format '{{json .}}'` has been changed to `time`, and its value is now a Unix timestamp in seconds. 
Apart from that, a `timeNano` field is also introduced, which is the same as the time field but in nanoseconds precision.
```

comments.body:
> You broke some tests.

Yes, going to mark this WIP until I find the root cause.

> One issue is this could be a breaking change. 

Yes, that is a breaking change.  I'm OK going forward but I won't find time reviewing; I'll be on PTO starting tomorrow.

Suggestion for system tests:
```diff
diff --git a/test/system/090-events.bats b/test/system/090-events.bats
index 7be7f69c1..f96146517 100644
--- a/test/system/090-events.bats
+++ b/test/system/090-events.bats
@@ -224,9 +224,9 @@ EOF
     cat $eventsFile
     CONTAINERS_CONF_OVERRIDE=$containersConf run_podman events --stream=false --since="2022-03-06T11:26:42.723667984+02:00" --format=json
     assert "${#lines[@]}" = 52 "Number of events returned"
-    is "${lines[0]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"begin\"}}"
-    is "${lines[-2]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"end\"}}"
-    is "${lines[-1]}" "{\"ID\":\"$ctrID\",\"Image\":\"$IMAGE\",\"Name\":\".*\",\"Status\":\"remove\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"container\",\"Attributes\":{.*}}"
+    is "${lines[0]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"begin\"}}"
+    is "${lines[-2]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"end\"}}"
+    is "${lines[-1]}" "{\"ID\":\"$ctrID\",\"Image\":\"$IMAGE\",\"Name\":\".*\",\"Status\":\"remove\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"container\",\"Attributes\":{.*}}"
 }
 
 @test "events log-file no duplicates" {
@@ -283,20 +283,20 @@ EOF
     # Make sure that the log file has been rotated as expected.
     run cat $eventsFile
     assert "${#lines[@]}" = 54 "Number of events in $eventsFile" # 49 previous + 2 rotation + pull/create/rm
-    is "${lines[0]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"begin\"}}"
-    is "${lines[1]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723667951,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
-    is "${lines[49]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723667999,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
-    is "${lines[50]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"end\"}}"
-    is "${lines[53]}" "{\"ID\":\"$ctrID\",\"Image\":\"$IMAGE\",\"Name\":\".*\",\"Status\":\"remove\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"container\",\"Attributes\":{.*}}"
+    is "${lines[0]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"begin\"}}"
+    is "${lines[1]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
+    is "${lines[49]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
+    is "${lines[50]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"end\"}}"
+    is "${lines[53]}" "{\"ID\":\"$ctrID\",\"Image\":\"$IMAGE\",\"Name\":\".*\",\"Status\":\"remove\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"container\",\"Attributes\":{.*}}"
 
 
     # Make sure that the JSON stream looks as expected. That means it has all
     # events and no duplicates.
     run cat $eventsJSON
-    is "${lines[0]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723667900,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
-    is "${lines[99]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723667999,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
-    is "${lines[100]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"end\"}}"
-    is "${lines[103]}" "{\"ID\":\"$ctrID\",\"Image\":\"$IMAGE\",\"Name\":\".*\",\"Status\":\"remove\",\"time\":\".*\",\"Type\":\"container\",\"Attributes\":{.*}}"
+    is "${lines[0]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
+    is "${lines[99]}" "{\"Name\":\"busybox\",\"Status\":\"pull\",\"time\":1649237202,\"timeNano\":1649237202723[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"image\",\"Attributes\":null}"
+    is "${lines[100]}" "{\"Name\":\"$eventsFile\",\"Status\":\"log-rotation\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"system\",\"Attributes\":{\"io.podman.event.rotate\":\"end\"}}"
+    is "${lines[103]}" "{\"ID\":\"$ctrID\",\"Image\":\"$IMAGE\",\"Name\":\".*\",\"Status\":\"remove\",\"time\":[0-9]\+,\"timeNano\":[0-9]\+,\"Type\":\"container\",\"Attributes\":{.*}}"
 }
 
 # Prior to #15633, container labels would not appear in 'die' log events
```
(this does not constitute a review; it's only a drive-by hint to help get tests passing, to then enable review)
Thanks @edsantiago 👍. It's ready for review now,

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *AhmedGrati*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@Luap99 okay then, if no objections I'll work on that!

> The better way to fix this is to have a proper cli frontend struct in cmd/podman/system/events.go that you can convert the libpod event to and then display it only on the cli level.

@Luap99 looking into this, I think we'll need to have duplicate `event` structures. The one already in the `Libpod` API and the other is in the `cmd` level, which means that we'll have some sort of dependencies between these structures where any change made to the `Libpod` event should be reflected in the `cmd` event which can be hard to maintain. please correct me if I'm missing something. wdyt?

Yes there is duplication for most of the fields. But this brings in order advantages, having a proper stable cli struct which we then no longer break by accident (hopefully). It also means the we can have different data, for example the internal struct uses one time field while the cli one can use two.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
@Luap99 Can you take a look at this? (Any hint about the issue?)

try with

```diff
diff --git a/cmd/podman/system/events.go b/cmd/podman/system/events.go
index d5635599e..ec398ee9d 100644
--- a/cmd/podman/system/events.go
+++ b/cmd/podman/system/events.go
@@ -163,7 +163,7 @@ func eventsFlags(cmd *cobra.Command) {
 
        formatFlagName := "format"
        flags.StringVar(&eventFormat, formatFlagName, "", "format the output using a Go template")
-       _ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc(formatFlagName, common.AutocompleteFormat(&events.Event{}))
+       _ = cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc(formatFlagName, common.AutocompleteFormat(&Event{}))
 
        flags.BoolVar(&eventOptions.Stream, "stream", true, "stream events and do not exit when returning the last known event")
```
@rhatdan @Luap99 the main account is currently locked... I'll return to work on this once unlocked (hopefully very soon...)

I am sorry to hear this. I take it over, rebase and add some small changes. We want to get this in by today.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21784
